title: Josef Grunger
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:GAGrunger-SWI66.png]] |name=Josef Grunger |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]],''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' [[Aggressor (Executor-class)|''Aggressor'']], near [[Tralus/Legends|Tralus]]{{InsiderCite|66|Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals}} |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him''[[The Glove of Darth Vader]]'' |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Green]] |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] **[[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] *[[Grunger's armada]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Lord Trioculus, are you aware that there are others who claim to be the new ruler of the Empire? I have been to the planet Gargon. There, Grand Admiral Grunger says ''he'' is our new leader. And ''he'' has a fleet of thirty star destroyers!|A member of the Royal Guard, to Trioculus|}} '''Josef Grunger''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] serving in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Navy]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. A [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]], Grunger was one of the twelve original beings elevated to the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] [[2 BBY/Legends|two years before]] the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Neither subtle nor loyal by nature, Grunger nonetheless remained devoted to Palpatine until [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], when the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] was [[Death/Legends|killed]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. The Grand [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]], having been busy patrolling [[Realspace/Legends|space]] near the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Gargon/Legends|Gargon]], seized the world when he received the news of the defeat at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. Almost immediately, he set himself up as a [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] and declared that he was the new Emperor. By [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], Grunger had amassed a sizable [[Fleet/Legends|armada]] of [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] and smaller [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]], using the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Aggressor (Executor-class)|''Aggressor'']] as his [[Command ship/Legends|command ship]]. News of Grunger's claim to the vacant throne came to the [[Central Committee of Grand Moffs]] on [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]]. Their leader, the [[mutant]] [[Supreme Slavelord|Slavelord]] [[Trioculus]], vowed to deal with Grunger after receiving the [[dark blessing]] of the [[Supreme Prophet]] of the Dark Side [[Rajah Ubooki|Kadann]], which the [[Slavery/Legends|slavelord]] required to be named Emperor. Grunger doubted Trioculus' claims but nonetheless vowed to follow the mutant if he received Kadann's approval. Trioculus died not long after obtaining the blessing, however, and Grunger—having retained full control of his armada—became a warlord once more. Plotting a strike at the former [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the Grand Admiral moved his forces into the [[Corellian sector/Legends|Corellian sector]]. Above the planet [[Tralus/Legends|Tralus]] in the [[Corellian system/Legends|Corellian system]], Grunger's fleet was [[Battle of Tralus (Galactic Civil War)|confronted]] by Grand Admiral [[Danetta Pitta]], who took command of a [[Grand Admiral Pitta's torpedo sphere|Torpedo Sphere]] and used it to annihilate his opponent's armada. Faced with defeat, Grunger [[Ramming/Legends|rammed]] the ''Aggressor'' into the Torpedo Sphere, obliterating both [[Warship/Legends|warships]] and killing both Grand Admirals. ==Biography== ===Grand Admiral=== [[File:PalpatineandGrandAdmirals.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Grunger meets with Emperor Palpatine and other Grand Admirals.]] Josef Grunger was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as an [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|navy]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Over the course of his career, he became a highly-regarded strategist. In [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], during a [[Fete Week|New Year's Fete Week]] celebration on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], he was one of twelve naval [[Imperial officer/Legends|officers]] elevated to the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], while fellow Grand [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Miltin Takel]] traveled to the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] to witness what was supposed to be the destruction of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], Grunger was assisting Takel's [[Unidentified Imperial first officer|second-in-command]] in patrolling the [[Mandalore sector/Legends|Mandalore sector]]—specifically [[Realspace/Legends|space]] near the [[Spice/Legends|spice]]-producing [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Gargon/Legends|Gargon]]—due to reports of "[[Nagai/Legends|albino-skinned marauders]]."{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/nagai11202009/index.html|text=The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs|int=The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs}} When word reached Grunger of the Imperial [[Battle of Endor/Legends|defeat]] at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] and the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] [[Death/Legends|death]], the Grand Admiral had his [[Starship/Legends|ship]] open fire on the vessel of Takel's [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]], disabling it. Grunger seized Gargon and quickly set himself up as one of the first Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]]. Before long, he announced that he was the new Emperor. ===Imperial warlord=== {{Quote|My Dark Lordship, Grand Admiral Grunger still refuses to accept your claim to be Emperor—that is until Kadann, as Supreme Prophet of the Dark Side, officially gives you his dark blessing. In that case, Grunger will withdraw his objections and will order his fleet of Star Destroyers to follow your command.''"
"''And what is his excuse for withholding his loyalty?''"
"''Like so many of the others, my Lordship, he doubts your claim to be the son of Emperor Palpatine.|Grand Moff Hissa and Trioculus|''[[The Lost City of the Jedi]]''}} The Grand Admiral, using his rank to bully Imperial [[Officer/Legends|officers]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] into submission, quickly amassed a large [[Fleet/Legends|armada]] of [[Warship/Legends|warships]]. By [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], Grunger was in possession of thirty [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class]] and [[Victory I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyers]], one hundred [[Carrack-class light cruiser/Legends|''Carrack''-class cruisers]], and sixty [[Strike-class medium cruiser|''Strike''-class cruisers]], among other craft. Grunger led this fleet from the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] [[Aggressor (Executor-class)|''Aggressor'']], his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]]. Having been ousted from his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of Gargon, Grand Admiral Takel eventually allied himself with the [[Central Committee of Grand Moffs]], hoping that they would be able to deal with Grunger. Meanwhile, a [[Unidentified Royal Guard|member]] of the [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Imperial Royal Guard]], having been to Gargon, brought news of Grunger's claim to the throne to the Central Committee on [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]]. During an assembly in [[Kessendra Stadium]] in which [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Bertroff Hissa]] announced the [[mutant]] [[Supreme Slavelord|Slavelord]] [[Trioculus]] to be Palpatine's son and heir, the Royal Guard questioned the validity of Trioculus' claim over Grunger's, noting that the Grand Admiral had amassed a sizable force of [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]]. The [[Slavery/Legends|slavelord]] declared that he would deal with Grunger in time. In a committee meeting that followed, Trioculus demanded that the [[glove of Darth Vader]] be found, which would entitle him to the [[dark blessing]] of [[Supreme Prophet]] of the Dark Side [[Kadann]]—something he needed in order to secure the vacant throne. Takel feared that Grunger would retrieve the glove first and therefore make good on his claim to the throne, but Takel was quickly executed by Hissa for questioning Trioculus' plans. Hissa later brought news to Trioculus that Grunger, doubting the mutant's claim of being Palpatine's son, would only be willing to support Trioculus after he received the dark blessing from Kadann, who was in truth an [[Rajah Ubooki|imposter]] posing as the true Supreme Prophet.''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' At that point, Grunger promised to turn over command of his fleet to Trioculus. Though Trioculus did receive the dark blessing from the false Supreme Prophet, his reign as Emperor was short-lived; he died not long after taking the title.[[Prophets of the Dark Side (novel)|''Prophets of the Dark Side'']] Grunger, meanwhile, had avoided any permanent ties with the Central Committee and retained control of his armada. In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], following the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] execution of Grand Admiral [[Osvald Teshik]] a year earlier, Grunger prepared to strike at Imperial Center. To do so, Grunger moved his armada into the [[Corellian sector/Legends|Corellian sector]]. However, Grand Admiral [[Danetta Pitta]] had bribed the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Diktat|leader]] into relinquishing control of the [[Sector/Legends|sector]], and Pitta quickly assumed command. When Grunger's fleet, led by the ''Aggressor'', emerged from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] in the [[Corellian system/Legends|Corellian system]] near the planet [[Tralus/Legends|Tralus]], Pitta took control of a [[Grand Admiral Pitta's torpedo sphere|Torpedo Sphere]] and began to [[Battle of Tralus (Galactic Civil War)|annihilate]] his rival's forces. Faced with defeat and the loss of his pride, Grunger had his [[Command ship/Legends|command ship]] [[Ramming/Legends|ram]] the Torpedo Sphere. The resulting collision destroyed both vessels, and both Grunger and his opponent perished. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Grand Admirals Grunger and Pitta turned warlord and annihilated each other in a bitter and ultimately futile fight for control of the Corellian sector.|[[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]], reporting on the Grand Admiral's fate|''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''}} Grunger was neither a loyal nor a subtle man, turning warlord almost immediately after receiving news of the Emperor's death at the Battle of Endor. Swiftly seizing Gargon, Grunger sought to amass a large armada, and the Grand Admiral effectively employed deception and outright attacks on fellow Imperials to accomplish this goal. Before long, he had obtained a sizable fleet of Star Destroyers and declared himself the new Emperor. Despite his claim to the throne, Grunger declared that he would fully support the Slavelord Trioculus, backed by the Central Committee of Grand Moffs, if the mutant obtained both the glove of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] and the dark blessing of the Supreme Prophet Kadann. Trioculus received both, but Grunger avoided any permanent alliance with the Central Committee and retained command of his fleet following the mutant's death. Ultimately, Grunger's pride led to his death; when faced with defeat at the Battle of Tralus, he opted to ram Pitta's Torpedo Sphere—a move that killed them both—rather than lose to his opponent. ==Behind the scenes== Grand Admiral Josef Grunger was first mentioned in the [[1992]] young adult novel ''[[The Glove of Darth Vader]]'', written by [[Paul Davids|Paul]] and [[Hollace Davids]]. ''Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals'' was originally intended for inclusion in ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine, before it was canceled, under the title "''[[Dirty Dozen: The Imperial Grand Admirals]]''." When the article was reused in ''Star Wars Insider'', several details on the Grand Admirals were excluded. Grunger's section of the article remained relatively intact, though a few details were omitted.{{Blog|danwallace|16|Grand Admirals: What Have They Got in their Pocketsses?}}"[[Dirty Dozen: The Imperial Grand Admirals]]" {{Cutstart}} ===Cut content=== Before the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Declaration of a New Order|transitioned]] into the Galactic Empire, Josef Grunger was an Alderaanian who served as a [[Republic marine]] and weapons specialist.{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=169992&start=132|thread=The other Imperial Grand Admirals…|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad]]|date=2005-01-26|time=2:46 PM}} As such, he was proficient with many types of hand-held weapons, ranging from [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifles]] to [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblades]]. He wielded a [[Riot gun/Legends|riot gun]] and possessed a [[Code cylinder/Legends|code cylinder]] and a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]]. Following the Battle of Endor, and before [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] had finished transmitting the news of the Emperor's death, Grunger had ordered his weapons officer to disable the ship of Grand Admiral Takel's subordinate using [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]]. Grunger quickly amassed a large armada, at first utilizing the communications disruption in the Outer Rim to dupe Imperial commanders into turning over their vessels to him. After establishing his reputation as a warlord, Grunger no longer required such deceptive moves to seize ships, merely taking whatever he wanted. {{Cutend}} ==Appearances== *''[[The Glove of Darth Vader]]'' {{1stm}} *''[[The Lost City of the Jedi]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Josef Grunger}} *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] *''[[Rebellion Era Sourcebook]]'' *{{InsiderCite|66|Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals}} {{1stID|Josef}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/nagai11202009/index.html|text=The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs|int=The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs}} *[[Star Wars: The Official Starships & Vehicles Collection 67|''Star Wars: The Official Starships & Vehicles Collection'' 67]] *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' *{{SW|url=news/the-star-wars-spy-game-spin-declassified|text=The ''Star Wars'' Spy Game: SPIN Declassified|int=The Star Wars Spy Game: SPIN Declassified|nolive=1}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{GrandAdmiralsNav}} {{Interlang |de=Josef Grunger |es=Josef Grunger }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Grunger, Josef}} [[Category:Grand Admirals of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial warlords]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]]
